assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 9-17 show upstream backport task with labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"]. Lines 140-152 show jira.create_link with type: "Depend" linking both upstream and downstream tasks to TC-8020. Downstream task (lines 77-85) also uses the same standard labels.

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 164-168: 'The cross-stream version impact analysis reveals that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected (tokio 1.40.0, threshold 1.42.0). A JQL search for sibling CVE Jiras with label CVE-2026-55123 returns no results for stream rhtpa-2.1 -- no CVE Jira exists for that stream. Per Step 8 Case B, proactive preemptive remediation tasks are created for stream rhtpa-2.1.' Two preemptive tasks (upstream backport and downstream propagation) are then fully described at lines 170-306.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 179: labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"] for preemptive upstream task. Line 250: identical labels for preemptive downstream task. Also confirmed in cross-stream.md lines 57 and 92.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 310-311: 'Preemptive tasks use "Related" link type (not "Depend") to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020, because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream (rhtpa-2.2)'. Lines 314-317 and 320-323 show jira.create_link calls with type: "Related" for both preemptive tasks. Cross-stream.md lines 76-81 and 103-107 confirm the same.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 195-199: '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). > No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Lines 267-270 contain the identical prefix for the downstream propagation subtask. Cross-stream.md lines 67-70 also document this prefix.
